root_cause.text: The `CronTab` class fails to restrict calendar field values to their actual maximums when setting them, causing date calculations (such as `floor`) to enter an infinite loop.
root_cause.evidence: The patch modifies `CronTab.java` in the `setTo` method to cap the set value using `Math.min(i-offset, c.getActualMaximum(field))`, and the added test case demonstrates that evaluating a cron expression like `"0 0 31 7 *"` previously resulted in an infinite loop.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The `CronTab` class fails to restrict calendar field values to their actual maximums when setting them, causing date calculations (such as `floor`) to enter an infinite loop.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch modifies `CronTab.java` in the `setTo` method to cap the set value using `Math.min(i-offset, c.getActualMaximum(field))`, and the added test case demonstrates that evaluating a cron expression like `"0 0 31 7 *"` previously resulted in an infinite loop.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a boundary check to prevent setting calendar fields beyond their maximum allowed values.
intent_record.what: Modified the assignment to cap the value at the calendar field's maximum.
intent_record.how: Prevents out-of-bounds date values by limiting the input to the actual maximum allowed for the specific calendar field.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a test case to ensure CronTab.floor() does not infinite loop for specific month and day combinations.
intent_record.what: Added a new test method `testLongMonths` with a timeout.
intent_record.how: Verifies that the `floor` method does not get stuck in an infinite loop for a specific date expression.

grouping_result.intent: Add a boundary check to prevent setting calendar fields beyond their maximum allowed values.
Add a test case to ensure CronTab.floor() does not infinite loop for specific month and day combinations.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly addresses the root cause by adding the missing boundary check to restrict calendar field values to their maximums.
The group adds a test case that directly reproduces and verifies the fix for the infinite loop vulnerability in CronTab.floor() caused by invalid calendar values.

candidates.rationale: This line sets the calendar field to a value without checking if it exceeds the actual maximum, which causes the infinite loop described in the root cause.

vuln_statements.explanation: This line sets the calendar field without ensuring it is bounded by the field's actual maximum, which directly leads to the infinite loop when calculating dates.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
added a method to compute the nearest matching time, given a time.

git-svn-id: https://hudson.dev.java.net/svn/hudson/t
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 350bf691654b9d2700322964179b155b02c83c14
Author: kohsuke <kohsuke@71c3de6d-444a-0410-be80-ed276b4c234a>
Date:   Thu Sep 9 22:36:44 2010 +0000

    added a method to compute the nearest matching time, given a time.
    
    git-svn-id: https://hudson.dev.java.net/svn/hudson/trunk/hudson/main@34633 71c3de6d-444a-0410-be80-ed276b4c234a

diff --git a/core/src/main/java/hudson/scheduler/CronTab.java b/core/src/main/java/hudson/scheduler/CronTab.java
index 3f4c8b899e..3031040d69 100644
--- a/co
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces the `CalendarField` class and the `ceil` method without bounds checking against the calendar field's actual maximums. In `setTo`, it simply calls `c.set(field,i-offset);`, which allows setting invalid dates (like February 31st) and can lead to infinite loops.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit e9276d8795eb98a71a530d4ab903fc6aa236a84b
Author: kohsuke <kohsuke@71c3de6d-444a-0410-be80-ed276b4c234a>
Date:   Sat Mar 15 22:00:06 2008 +0000

    improved error diagnostics of cron tab (#1406)
    
    
    git-svn-id: https://hudson.dev.java.net/svn/hudson/trunk/hudson/main@7782 71c3de6d-444a-0410-be80-ed276b4c234a

diff --git a/core/src/main/java/hudson/scheduler/CronTab.java b/core/src/main/java/hudson/scheduler/CronTab.java
index 6eee0da586..7390e763b0 100644
--- a/core/src/main/jav
bic_trace_step.reason: The `CronTab` class lacks logic to enforce upper bounds on calendar fields when they are set (e.g., in the `set` method or during parsing). The `UPPER_BOUNDS` array is only used for warnings in `checkSanity()`, leaving the class vulnerable to invalid inputs that can cause infinite loops during date calculations.
